methods.description: Collection and preparation of various geological samples from the Branca Opala cave and nearby areas for mineralogical and geochemical analysis.

steps.description: Various types of opaline stromatolite, silicified plant remains, fine volcaniclastic sediments, volcanic host rock and silica-tufa from the Branca Opala cave, and nearby in the Ribeira da Biscoitos area, were sampled.
steps.searchable_summary: collect geological samples from specified locations

steps.description: Only a limited number of speleothem samples were collected to avoid excessive damage to the decoration of the cave.
steps.searchable_summary: limit speleothem sample collection to minimize cave damage

methods.description: Determination of the mineralogical composition of samples using optical microscopy and X-ray diffraction (XRD) analysis.

steps.description: The mineralogical composition of the samples was determined by standard optical microscopy and X-ray diffraction (XRD) analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform optical microscopy and XRD analysis on samples

steps.description: Powder XRD patterns were obtained using pressed powder mounts employing a Philips semi-automatic PW 1710 diffractor producing monochromatized CuKa radiation.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain powder XRD patterns using Philips diffractor

steps.description: The oriented aggregates technique was used to study very fine sediments. Minus-2-micron fractions were mounted on glass microscope slides, saturated with glycol and heated to 450°C to generate diffraction data.
steps.searchable_summary: use oriented aggregates technique for fine sediments

methods.description: Basic petrological analyses and microstructure description using transmitted-light microscopy.

steps.description: Transmitted-light microscopy was used for basic petrological analyses and microstructure description.
steps.searchable_summary: perform transmitted-light microscopy for petrological analysis

steps.description: Thin sections of representative parts of each sample were examined under polarized light.
steps.searchable_summary: examine thin sections under polarized light

methods.description: Observation and analysis of small fractured samples using scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and energy dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDX).

steps.description: Small fractured samples of the collected material were mounted on stubs and coated with a layer of gold for observation using a FEI INSPECT scanning electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for SEM by mounting and gold coating

steps.description: Observation using a FEI INSPECT scanning electron microscope (SEM; 30 kV, distance 10 mm, high vacuum) equipped with secondary electron and backscatter detectors and an Oxford ANALYTICAL-INCA Xray energy dispersive system (EDX).
steps.searchable_summary: use SEM and EDX for sample observation

steps.description: Precise aluminium and silicon determinations were obtained by SEM using a FEI QUANTA 200 machine equipped with a wavelength dispersive spectrometer (WDS).
steps.searchable_summary: determine aluminium and silicon using SEM with WDS

methods.description: Geochemical analyses of whole-rock powder for major, minor and rare earth elements using ICP-ES and ICP-MS.

steps.description: Geochemical analyses of whole-rock powder for major, minor and rare earth elements (REE) were performed at Acme Analytical Laboratories using inductively coupled emission spectrometry (ICP-ES) and inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS).
steps.searchable_summary: perform ICP-ES and ICP-MS for geochemical analysis

steps.description: Classical whole-rock analyses were performed using a lithium borate fusion and dilute acid digestion of 0.2 g samples.
steps.searchable_summary: perform lithium borate fusion and acid digestion

steps.description: Loss on ignition (LOI) was determined by weight difference after ignition at 1000°C at the same laboratories.
steps.searchable_summary: determine LOI by weight difference after ignition
